animeApocalypseCodes.title: Anime Apocalypse Codes
animeApocalypseCodes.subtitle: Redeem active Anime Apocalypse codes for coins, ability spins, gadget spins, boost timers, raid tickets, and trait rerolls. Use these rewards before heavy early-game rolling so your first runs start with stronger abilities and gadgets.
animeApocalypseCodes.intro: This Anime Apocalypse codes module is built for players who want current reward codes and a clear redemption flow. Active codes are ordered first, followed by expired codes that players commonly search for after release and testing milestones.
animeApocalypseCodes.redeemTitle: How to Redeem Anime Apocalypse Codes
redeemSteps: Click the Codes icon on the right side of the screen.
Paste the code into the text box.
Press Confirm to claim the rewards.

items.bestUse: Claim this Anime Apocalypse code on a new or private server before spending early spins so the release milestone rewards can support your first ability and gadget rolls.

items.rewardSummary: 10k Coins, 5 Normal Ability Spins, 3 Normal Gadget Spins, 4 Lucky Ability Spins, 4 Lucky Gadget Spins, and x2 Coins for 1800 minutes
items.bestUse: Use this Anime Apocalypse code before a long farming session so the coin boost runs while you clear waves and build currency for upgrades.

items.rewardSummary: 10k Coins, 5 Normal Ability Spins, 3 Normal Gadget Spins, 4 Lucky Ability Spins, 4 Lucky Gadget Spins, and x2 Drops for 900 minutes
items.bestUse: Redeem this Anime Apocalypse code before dungeon runs or early item farming because the drop boost works best when you are actively clearing rooms.

items.rewardSummary: 15k Coins, 20 Raid Tickets, 5 Normal Ability Spins, 4 Normal Gadget Spins, 4 Lucky Ability Spins, 4 Lucky Gadget Spins, x2 Drops for 1200 minutes, and x2 Coins for 1200 minutes
items.bestUse: Save this Anime Apocalypse bundle for a focused progression block because it combines currency, raid access, spins, and both major farming boosts.

items.rewardSummary: 3 Lucky Gadget Spins and 10 Normal Gadget Spins
items.bestUse: Archived Anime Apocalypse release code for players checking older launch rewards.

items.rewardSummary: 8 Lucky Ability Spins and 5 Normal Ability Spins
items.bestUse: Archived Anime Apocalypse testing code for players checking pre-release or launch-week reward lists.
Archived Anime Apocalypse milestone code for players searching trait reroll rewards.

animeApocalypseBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Beginner Guide
animeApocalypseBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start from the lobby, clear zombie waves, choose useful cards, and spend your first rewards on the systems that matter most. This Anime Apocalypse guide turns the first few runs into a simple progression path.
animeApocalypseBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse is a Roblox action RPG where players fight zombie waves across anime-inspired maps, earn loot, roll for abilities and gadgets, and strengthen runs with cards, traits, pets, cosmetics, and upgrades. The best early path is to learn movement, clear accessible rooms, use codes for spins, and build around cooldown speed, economy, and consistent wave clear.

items.title: Spawn in the Anime Apocalypse lobby and open the play area
items.goal: Understand the basic Anime Apocalypse flow before spending spins or currency.
items.details: You begin in the Anime Apocalypse lobby with a starting ability, then enter the play area to select a map and begin clearing zombie-filled levels.
actions: Pick an early map instead of jumping straight into hard content.
Start a run to learn the room flow and enemy pacing.
items.beginnerTip: Use the first Anime Apocalypse run to learn where enemies spawn and how exits open after rooms are cleared.

items.title: Learn the Anime Apocalypse core controls
items.goal: Make Anime Apocalypse combat and movement feel automatic before harder waves.
items.details: Basic attack is handled with LMB, active skills are mapped to Z, X, C, and V, gadgets use E, ultimate uses G, dash uses Q, and slide uses Ctrl.
actions: Chain double jump and air dash for repositioning.
Use skills in rotation instead of saving every cooldown for bosses.
items.beginnerTip: Fast movement is a major Anime Apocalypse advantage because many rooms reward quick repositioning and clean wave clearing.

items.title: Choose an Anime Apocalypse beginner-friendly mode and map
items.goal: Clear Anime Apocalypse runs consistently before chasing harder rewards.
items.details: Survival-style dungeon runs are the main early learning path, while Infinite mode becomes better once you understand movement, cards, and cooldown cycles. Shibuya is the easier launch map, while Impel Down adds more demanding room layouts and visual hazards.
actions: Start with Shibuya for cleaner room flow.
Move to Impel Down after you are comfortable with dash and wall movement.
Use Infinite mode when you want wave practice and long-session farming.
items.beginnerTip: Do not panic around lava-style sections in Anime Apocalypse Impel Down; some sections are traversable and are part of the map flow.

items.title: Clear Anime Apocalypse waves and pick the right cards
items.goal: Turn each Anime Apocalypse run into a stronger build instead of relying only on your base ability.
items.details: After boss or wave milestones, Anime Apocalypse can offer cards that change a run with bonuses such as wealth, extra lives, cooldown reduction, and drop chance boosts.
actions: Pick Wealth early when you need more economy.
Shift into cooldown reduction once your currency flow is stable.
Use extra lives for safety in longer runs and Infinite mode.
items.beginnerTip: Cooldown cards become more valuable once your Anime Apocalypse ability rotation is strong enough to clear rooms quickly.

items.title: Spend early Anime Apocalypse rewards on spins and upgrades
items.goal: Improve your Anime Apocalypse build before trying to brute-force harder content.
items.details: Coins, spins, gadgets, traits, pets, and cosmetics all feed into progression, but early players should focus on ability and gadget access plus upgrades that help room clearing.
actions: Redeem active Anime Apocalypse codes before heavy rolling.
Use Normal and Lucky Ability Spins to expand your ability options.
Prioritize cooldown-speed upgrades, then movement and economy support.
items.beginnerTip: Cooldown speed is a high-priority Anime Apocalypse upgrade because faster skill cycling improves both wave clear and boss pressure.

items.title: Build around one reliable Anime Apocalypse ability first
items.goal: Avoid wasting Anime Apocalypse resources by constantly swapping before your first stable build is online.
items.details: Abilities are the primary combat system, while gadgets add a separate active tool. Early players should keep a consistent ability long enough to learn its range, cooldowns, damage pattern, ultimate timing, and awakening timing.
actions: Use Z, X, C, and V as your main rotation.
Save G for high-value ultimate moments.
Pair crowd-control gadgets with abilities that need grouped enemies.
items.beginnerTip: A consistent Anime Apocalypse ability with clean cooldown usage is stronger than a rare pull that you do not understand yet.

animeApocalypseAbilityTierList.title: Anime Apocalypse Ability Tier List
animeApocalypseAbilityTierList.subtitle: Compare the best Anime Apocalypse abilities for early farming, boss rooms, Infinite mode, and late-game clearing. Rankings focus on performance, summon value, mobility, cooldown efficiency, and how practical each ability is to use.
animeApocalypseAbilityTierList.intro: Abilities are the main combat tool in Anime Apocalypse, and every ability uses a skill kit built around active skills, ultimate timing, and awakening value. The strongest early choices are consistent wave clearers, while the strongest long-term chase is the rare Supreme-tier option.

abilities.name: Saiyan
abilities.rankingReason: Saiyan is the rarest Supreme-tier Anime Apocalypse ability and has the strongest raw performance, combining major walk-speed value with high damage output.
abilities.tradeoff: The 0.1% Supreme pull rate makes Saiyan a long-term Anime Apocalypse target rather than the ability most players should plan around on day one.

abilities.name: Shadow Monarch
abilities.rankingReason: Shadow Monarch is a popular Anime Apocalypse early choice because its Arise-style summon mechanic creates pressure while the player moves through zombie waves.
abilities.tradeoff: Shadow Monarch gets the most Anime Apocalypse value when you keep enemies under control and let summons contribute throughout the room.

abilities.name: Coyote
abilities.rankingReason: Coyote is one of the most popular Anime Apocalypse early choices thanks to consistent damage output, wolf-type summons, and smooth move animations.
abilities.tradeoff: Coyote rewards active Anime Apocalypse movement and clean skill rotation more than passive play.

abilities.name: Stark
abilities.rankingReason: Stark brings a gun-type Anime Apocalypse moveset with a strong area-of-effect ultimate, making it valuable when enemies are grouped or when a room needs burst damage.
abilities.tradeoff: Stark gets its best Anime Apocalypse value from timing the ultimate into dense enemy packs instead of using it on scattered targets.

abilities.name: King of Curses
abilities.rankingReason: King of Curses belongs to the Anime Apocalypse ability roster and works as a strong main-combat option when players want a full offensive kit before chasing the rarest pulls.
abilities.tradeoff: King of Curses performance depends on learning the full Anime Apocalypse skill sequence, ultimate timing, and awakening usage.

abilities.name: Sosuke
abilities.rankingReason: Sosuke is part of the Anime Apocalypse gacha ability pool and fits players who want a primary ability for regular runs while building toward higher-value chase options.
abilities.tradeoff: Sosuke should be evaluated by Anime Apocalypse cooldown flow and room clear consistency rather than name value alone.

abilities.name: Nen Prodigy
abilities.rankingReason: Nen Prodigy gives Anime Apocalypse players another listed ability route for building a stable kit before committing premium spins to rarer targets.
abilities.tradeoff: Nen Prodigy is best used as a learned Anime Apocalypse main ability, not as a temporary roll that gets replaced before mastery develops.

abilities.name: Zorro
abilities.rankingReason: Zorro is the default Anime Apocalypse starter weapon available to players, making it the natural first option for learning movement, targeting, and skill timing.
abilities.tradeoff: Move on once you roll a stronger, smoother, or more specialized Anime Apocalypse ability.

abilities.name: Fire Fighter
abilities.rankingReason: Fire Fighter is tied to Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass progression and has a flashy ultimate, but it clears dungeons more slowly than stronger practical picks.
abilities.tradeoff: Use Fire Fighter when you want the Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass weapon experience, not when you need the fastest clear speed.

animeApocalypseAbilitiesGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Abilities Guide
animeApocalypseAbilitiesGuide.subtitle: Learn how Anime Apocalypse abilities work, how to roll them, how Normal and Lucky Spins differ, and which known abilities are currently part of the launch roster. Use this before spending code rewards or premium spin bundles.
animeApocalypseAbilitiesGuide.intro: Abilities are the primary way to fight zombie waves in Anime Apocalypse. Each ability has three regular skills plus an Ultimate skill and an Awakening skill, while the Ability Gacha uses Normal Spins and Lucky Spins across Rare, Epic, Legendary, Mythic, and Supreme rarity categories.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Ability Skill Structure
items.details: Each Anime Apocalypse ability is built around three regular skills, an Ultimate skill, and an Awakening skill.
items.playerUse: Rotate regular skills for Anime Apocalypse wave clearing, save the Ultimate for boss rooms or dense enemy packs, and use Awakening when the fight needs a power spike.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha
items.details: Anime Apocalypse abilities are obtained by opening the Ability Gacha menu with E and rolling with Normal Spins or Lucky Spins.
controls: Lobby Ability Gacha: press E to open the rolling menu
items.playerUse: Use Normal Spins for broad Anime Apocalypse early rolls and Lucky Spins when you want a better chance at higher-rarity abilities.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Rarity Rates
items.details: Anime Apocalypse ability rarities are listed as Rare 62.1%, Epic 32%, Legendary 5%, Mythic 0.8%, and Supreme 0.1%.
items.playerUse: Treat Supreme abilities as long-term Anime Apocalypse chase pulls and build a stable run around the best ability you already own.

items.name: Anime Apocalypse Pity Rules
items.details: The Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha has pity milestones: 50 rolls guarantee a Legendary ability and 100 rolls guarantee a Mythic ability.
items.playerUse: Do not burn every Anime Apocalypse Lucky Spin immediately; plan bigger rolling sessions when your pity count and code rewards can work together.

items.name: Zorro
items.obtainMethod: Available as the Anime Apocalypse starter weapon for all players
items.details: Zorro is the practical first Anime Apocalypse ability for learning attacks, room movement, and basic skill rotation.
items.playerUse: Use Zorro for the first few Anime Apocalypse runs, then compare new rolls against its clear speed and comfort.

items.name: Nen Prodigy
items.details: Nen Prodigy is part of the known Anime Apocalypse ability roster and can be used as a main combat kit while progressing through early and mid-game rooms.
items.playerUse: Keep Nen Prodigy long enough to learn its Anime Apocalypse rotation before replacing it with a higher-performing pull.

items.name: Sosuke
items.details: Sosuke is one of the listed abilities in the Anime Apocalypse roster and works as a main ability option for regular clearing.
items.playerUse: Evaluate Sosuke by Anime Apocalypse cooldown flow, range comfort, and how well it clears grouped zombie waves.

items.name: King of Curses
items.details: King of Curses is a listed Anime Apocalypse ability option for players building around a stronger offensive kit.
items.playerUse: Use King of Curses as a main damage ability and learn where its Ultimate and Awakening give the highest value.

items.name: Coyote
items.details: Coyote uses wolf-type summons and smooth move animations, making it one of the most popular practical choices for Anime Apocalypse early progression.
items.playerUse: Pick Coyote when you want consistent Anime Apocalypse wave damage with strong mobility-friendly gameplay.

items.name: Shadow Monarch
items.details: Shadow Monarch is known for an Arise-style mechanic that summons wolf allies and provides consistent pressure during Anime Apocalypse zombie waves.
items.playerUse: Use Shadow Monarch for Anime Apocalypse early farming and sustained room clearing while you move aggressively around enemy groups.

items.name: Stark
items.details: Stark has a gun-type Anime Apocalypse moveset and a strong area-of-effect ultimate for burst damage.
items.playerUse: Save Stark ultimate for dense groups, boss pressure windows, or rooms where enemies can be pulled together by a gadget.

items.name: Saiyan
items.details: Saiyan is the rarest Supreme-tier Anime Apocalypse ability and the strongest raw-performance chase option, with major walk-speed and damage value.
items.playerUse: Treat Saiyan as a long-term Anime Apocalypse goal and avoid delaying progression while waiting for a 0.1% pull.

items.name: Fire Fighter
items.obtainMethod: Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass final-level reward
items.details: Fire Fighter is the Anime Apocalypse Battle Pass weapon and has a flashy ultimate, but its dungeon clear speed is slower than the strongest practical picks.
items.playerUse: Use Fire Fighter for Anime Apocalypse collection, Battle Pass completion value, and variety rather than maximum farming speed.

animeApocalypseGadgetsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadgets Guide for Safer Wave Control
animeApocalypseGadgetsGuide.subtitle: Use Anime Apocalypse gadgets as secondary active skills that support your main ability loadout with crowd control, defense, enemy grouping, and safer wave clearing.
animeApocalypseGadgetsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse gadgets are separate from your main anime-inspired ability and give each run another tool for handling zombie pressure. Normal Gadget Spins and Lucky Gadget Spins help unlock gadget options, while strong picks such as Black Hole and Infinity can define how safely and quickly you clear dungeon rooms or survive Infinite mode.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadget Slot
items.description: The gadget slot acts as a secondary active skill that helps your ability handle zombie waves. A good gadget adds offense, defense, crowd control, or emergency protection depending on its effect.
items.bestFor: Players who want extra safety and faster wave control
notes: Runs alongside your main Anime Apocalypse ability
Supports both dungeon clearing and Infinite mode
Works best when picked to solve a clear weakness in your build

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Normal Gadget Spins
items.description: Normal Gadget Spins roll the gadget system through the same gacha-style progression loop used by other Anime Apocalypse upgrades. They are useful early because code rewards often help players build a starter gadget pool quickly.
items.bestFor: Early gadget collection and replacing weak starter rolls
notes: Good for building a first usable gadget pool
Commonly paired with code reward planning
Best spent before using rarer gadget resources

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Lucky Gadget Spins
items.description: Lucky Gadget Spins are more valuable because they support higher-rarity gadget hunting. Save them for sessions where you are actively trying to improve your gadget slot instead of casually rerolling between short runs.
items.bestFor: Chasing stronger gadgets such as Black Hole or Infinity
notes: Appears in major Anime Apocalypse release rewards
Best saved for focused reroll sessions
Most useful once you know your preferred playstyle

items.title: Black Hole
items.description: Black Hole pulls nearby enemies together, making it one of the most practical Anime Apocalypse gadgets for clearing dense zombie groups. It shines when your main ability has area damage or when dungeon rooms become crowded.
items.bestFor: Dungeon clearing, wave grouping, and farming speed
notes: Pairs well with fast cooldown rotations

items.title: Infinity
items.description: Infinity creates a protective bubble that stops enemies from reaching you for a short duration. It is especially strong in Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode because heavy enemy pressure makes defensive uptime valuable.
items.bestFor: Infinite mode, survival, and emergency defense
notes: Helps when enemies flood the screen
Combines well with extra-life and survival card choices

items.title: Time Stop
items.description: Time Stop gives a brief control window, but Anime Apocalypse rewards fast movement, repositioning, and quick wave clearing. It can still help defensive players, especially while learning enemy pacing.
items.bestFor: Situational control and slower defensive play
notes: Less efficient than Black Hole for fast clears
Better as a temporary control option than a final farming target

animeApocalypseGadgetTierList.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadget Tier List for Dungeon and Infinite Mode
animeApocalypseGadgetTierList.subtitle: Compare Anime Apocalypse gadgets by crowd control, defense, Infinite mode value, boss-room usefulness, and farming speed.
animeApocalypseGadgetTierList.intro: Gadgets change how an Anime Apocalypse run feels because they add a separate active tool on top of your main ability. Black Hole and Infinity are the strongest practical choices for crowd-heavy gameplay, while Time Stop is more situational because the game rewards fast movement and rapid room clearing.

items.name: Infinity
strengths: Very strong when enemies fill the screen
Excellent for Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode
items.keepOrReroll: Keep Infinity and build Anime Apocalypse survival runs around its defensive window.

items.name: Black Hole
items.keepOrReroll: Keep Black Hole, especially when your Anime Apocalypse ability has strong area damage.

items.name: High-control gadget slot
strengths: Useful when it groups enemies, prevents damage, or stabilizes boss rooms
Worth testing before spending rare rerolls
items.keepOrReroll: Keep temporarily if it clearly improves Anime Apocalypse safety or clear speed.

items.name: Time Stop
strengths: Can create a brief control window
items.keepOrReroll: Reroll Time Stop when you are chasing faster Anime Apocalypse farming or stronger Infinite mode survival.

items.name: Low-impact gadget rolls
strengths: Can help before you obtain a stronger gadget
Acceptable while saving Lucky Gadget Spins
items.keepOrReroll: Replace low-impact rolls once you can target a stronger Anime Apocalypse crowd-control or defensive gadget.

animeApocalypseTraitsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Traits Guide: When to Reroll and What to Keep
animeApocalypseTraitsGuide.subtitle: Traits are passive modifiers rolled onto Anime Apocalypse abilities to improve damage, movement, cooldown flow, or survivability.
animeApocalypseTraitsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse Trait Rerolls are valuable progression resources, so the best approach is to spend them on abilities you plan to keep. Strong traits matter most when attached to high-rarity or long-term abilities that already fit your farming, boss-room, or Infinite mode plan.

rows.whatItImproves: High-end offensive value for stronger Anime Apocalypse ability builds
rows.bestUseCase: Players building around a strong ability and looking for better damage performance
rows.rerollAdvice: Keep Reaper on a strong Anime Apocalypse ability and avoid replacing it casually.

rows.bestUseCase: Fast dungeon clears, mobile playstyles, and safer movement through crowded rooms
rows.rerollAdvice: Keep Flash if your Anime Apocalypse build benefits from speed and room-to-room tempo.

rows.whatItImproves: Wave clearing, boss damage, and farming speed
rows.bestUseCase: Main abilities used for dungeon grinding or high-pressure combat
rows.rerollAdvice: Spend rerolls here when the Anime Apocalypse ability is rare enough to keep long term.

rows.whatItImproves: Skill rotation speed and ability uptime
rows.bestUseCase: Abilities that rely on cycling multiple skills quickly
rows.rerollAdvice: Target cooldown value once your Anime Apocalypse economy and ability choice are stable.

rows.whatItImproves: Dodging, repositioning, and faster map flow
rows.bestUseCase: Infinite mode survival and speed-focused dungeon clears
rows.rerollAdvice: Keep movement value if it noticeably improves Anime Apocalypse survival or clear speed.

rows.bestUseCase: First few runs before you unlock better abilities
rows.rerollAdvice: Do not spend too many Anime Apocalypse Trait Rerolls here; save them for stronger abilities.

animeApocalypseCardsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Cards Guide for Better Run Choices
animeApocalypseCardsGuide.subtitle: Anime Apocalypse cards appear during a run and shape your temporary build with economy, cooldown, survivability, and drop bonuses.
animeApocalypseCardsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse uses a roguelike card layer between waves or after bosses, letting each run develop differently. Economy cards are powerful early, cooldown reduction improves wave-clearing speed once your build is stable, and extra lives become more valuable when survival pressure rises.

items.title: Wealth
items.description: Wealth increases the value of an Anime Apocalypse run by improving resource gain. It is one of the best early picks because faster economy helps you buy upgrades, roll more, and strengthen future attempts.
items.pickTiming: Prioritize Wealth early before your Anime Apocalypse economy is stable.

items.title: Cooldown Reduction
items.description: Cooldown reduction lets you cycle ability skills more often, which directly improves Anime Apocalypse wave clearing when your main ability already has strong damage.
items.pickTiming: Pick cooldown value after you have enough economy or when waves start getting dense.

items.title: Extra Lives
items.description: Extra lives give more room for mistakes during difficult rooms, boss fights, or long Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode runs. They are strongest when enemy density rises and one bad position can end the run.
items.pickTiming: Pick extra lives when Anime Apocalypse survival pressure becomes more important than raw farming speed.

items.title: Drop Chance Increase
items.description: Drop chance increases improve the reward side of an Anime Apocalypse run by helping you collect more useful drops from combat. It fits farming-focused dungeon runs where the goal is stronger progression value.
items.pickTiming: Pick drop chance when your Anime Apocalypse build already clears waves safely.

items.title: Coin Bonus
items.description: Coin-focused cards are useful because coins support upgrades and future rolls. They are especially good in Anime Apocalypse early progression when every run should improve permanent power.
items.pickTiming: Pick coin bonuses early while you still need core Anime Apocalypse upgrades.

items.title: Defensive Card Stack
items.description: Defensive picks become more valuable in Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode because waves keep scaling. Combining survival cards with a strong defensive gadget such as Infinity can make long runs much safer.
items.pickTiming: Pick defensive stacking once enemy pressure starts outpacing your Anime Apocalypse damage.

animeApocalypseInfiniteModeGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Infinite Mode Guide
animeApocalypseInfiniteModeGuide.subtitle: Push deeper into Anime Apocalypse endless zombie waves with better movement, card choices, cooldown cycling, and defensive gadget usage.
animeApocalypseInfiniteModeGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode keeps scaling after normal wave pressure would usually end. Stable long-run survival comes from reliable mobility, fast skill rotation, enemy grouping, defensive tools, and clean room-to-room pacing.

priorityItems.label: Best Starting Map
priorityItems.detail: Open lanes and readable room flow make Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode easier to control.

priorityItems.label: Core Combat Goal
priorityItems.detail: Pull zombie waves together before spending large ability skills, ultimates, or Black Hole.

priorityItems.label: Run Stability
priorityItems.detail: Cooldown reduction keeps damage available while defensive cards and Infinity protect mistake windows.

items.title: Start Anime Apocalypse Infinite Mode With Shibuya
items.description: Shibuya is the recommended Anime Apocalypse Infinite mode map because its open layout and straightforward room flow are easier to control at high speed. Map comfort matters more than raw challenge once waves keep scaling.

items.title: Use Anime Apocalypse Movement To Control The Wave
items.description: Chain double jump, air dash, wall run, dash, and slide to stay ahead of zombie groups. Strong movement lets you pull enemies into predictable paths before using AoE skills or a crowd-control gadget.

items.title: Group Anime Apocalypse Enemies Before Big Cooldowns
items.description: Avoid spending major skills on scattered zombies. Black Hole is valuable because it pulls enemies together, making ability skills and ultimates much more efficient for wave clearing.

items.title: Shift Anime Apocalypse Cards Toward Cooldown Reduction
items.description: Early Wealth cards help accelerate gold and resource gain, but cooldown reduction becomes more important once waves begin filling the screen. Faster skill cycling means fewer moments where you are only running away.

items.title: Keep Anime Apocalypse Defensive Tools Ready
items.description: Extra-life cards and defensive gadgets extend runs when enemy density becomes hard to manage. Infinity creates a protective bubble that helps recover when enemies reach your path.

items.title: Track Anime Apocalypse Stragglers And Keep Moving
items.description: Use the mini map to find enemies that fall behind your movement route. If a room feels stalled, watch for the exit direction prompt so the run does not slow down between waves.

animeApocalypseSurvivalModeGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Survival Mode Guide
animeApocalypseSurvivalModeGuide.subtitle: Learn how to clear Anime Apocalypse levels, survive zombie waves, handle boss rooms, and avoid losing progress from careless deaths.
animeApocalypseSurvivalModeGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse Survival mode is the main level-clearing loop. You select a map, fight through zombie waves, choose perk cards between key moments, collect loot, and use lobby upgrades, traits, abilities, pets, and gadgets to become stronger.

items.title: Prepare Your Anime Apocalypse Loadout In The Lobby
items.description: Use the lobby systems to roll abilities and gadgets, check traits, spend gold on useful upgrades, and equip the best available loadout before starting. Cooldown speed and walk speed upgrades make early clears smoother.
items.tip: Enter the run with a clear ability, a support gadget, and enough movement comfort to recover from crowded rooms.

items.title: Pick An Anime Apocalypse Map That Matches Your Comfort
items.description: Shibuya is easier to learn because it has a more open layout and simpler room flow. Impel Down works better after you want a harder dungeon feel with underground sections and lava-floor areas.
items.tip: Choose Shibuya for consistency first, then move into Impel Down once your build and movement are stable.

items.title: Clear Anime Apocalypse Waves With Movement First
items.description: Use dash, slide, double jump, air dash, and wall run to avoid surrounded positions. Once enemies are grouped, spend ability skills, gadget effects, and your ultimate to clear them quickly.
items.tip: Movement buys time; damage finishes the wave after zombies are already lined up.

items.title: Choose Anime Apocalypse Cards Based On Run Stage
items.description: Early in a run, Wealth is useful because it increases resource gain. As the run gets harder, cooldown reduction, drop chance, and extra-life cards become more important for clearing waves and surviving mistakes.
items.tip: Start with economy when safe, then swap toward cooldown and survival when rooms become crowded.

items.title: Play Anime Apocalypse Boss Rooms Safely
items.description: Deaths can send you back to the beginning of a dungeon, so slow down before boss rooms instead of rushing with low health or poorly timed cooldowns. Save major skills, ultimates, and defensive gadget windows for dangerous enemy spikes.
items.tip: Treat boss rooms as checkpoints where cooldown timing matters more than speed.

items.title: Collect Anime Apocalypse Pickups Before Leaving
items.description: Glowing ground items are easy to miss while moving quickly, so sweep rooms before leaving. The mini map helps track leftover enemies when one zombie prevents the next room or wave from starting.
items.tip: A short cleanup loop prevents missed loot and avoids stalled progression.

animeApocalypseMapsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Maps Guide
animeApocalypseMapsGuide.subtitle: Compare Anime Apocalypse maps by layout, difficulty, farming flow, movement routes, and Infinite mode value before starting a run.
animeApocalypseMapsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse maps are built around zombie wave clearing, room flow, and movement control. Shibuya and Impel Down play differently: one favors clean learning routes, while the other adds a more dangerous dungeon feel for players who want extra pressure.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya
items.description: Shibuya has a more open layout and straightforward room flow, making it easier for new players to learn enemy tracking, movement routes, and cooldown timing. It is also the preferred map for Infinite mode because the layout supports faster pacing.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Impel Down
items.description: Impel Down uses a prison-themed layout with underground sections and lava-floor visuals. It is better suited for players who already understand movement, enemy grouping, boss-room caution, and dungeon pacing.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Map Layout Impact
items.description: Open spaces make it easier to kite zombies, group enemies, and land AoE skills. More complex layouts slow down farming because you must watch corners, track stragglers, and avoid losing time between rooms.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Movement Routes
items.description: Double jump, air dash, wall run, dash, and slide matter more on maps with long rooms or vertical surfaces. Players who move well clear waves faster because enemies spend more time chasing instead of attacking.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Infinite Mode Choice
items.description: For endless waves, Shibuya is the safer recommendation because the layout makes enemy control easier. This lets players focus on cooldown cycling, defensive gadgets, and extra-life card management instead of fighting the map itself.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Dungeon Mode Choice
items.description: For normal dungeon clearing, Impel Down becomes a stronger pick once you want harder gameplay. Its layout and visual hazards make it a better test after you have upgraded your character and learned safer boss-room habits.

animeApocalypseShibuyaGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Guide
animeApocalypseShibuyaGuide.subtitle: Master Anime Apocalypse Shibuya with safer movement paths, better enemy grouping, and a build plan that works for early progression and Infinite mode.
animeApocalypseShibuyaGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya is one of the key early maps and the best starting point for learning the movement-based survival loop. Its open layout gives players more room to kite zombies, test gadgets, practice card choices, and build confidence before entering more demanding runs.

items.title: Why Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Is Beginner-Friendly
items.label: Easy Learning Curve
items.description: Shibuya has a cleaner, more open layout than Impel Down, so new players can see enemy movement more easily. This makes it a strong map for practicing wave control, dodging, and skill timing.

items.title: Best Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Use Case
items.label: Infinite Mode Practice
items.description: Shibuya is a strong Infinite mode pick because the faster pacing works well with its room flow. Players can focus on cooldown cycling and survival cards instead of getting slowed down by complex terrain.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Safe Movement Route
items.label: Kite First
items.description: Use wide loops around enemy groups, then cut across open space with dash or air dash when zombies close in. Wall running and double jumps help reset distance before using a major skill.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Enemy Grouping Plan
items.label: AoE Setup
items.description: Let zombies gather before using large attacks, ultimates, or Black Hole. Grouping enemies first makes every cooldown more valuable and reduces the time spent chasing scattered targets.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Card Priority
items.label: Run Stability
items.description: Choose Wealth early when farming, then shift into cooldown reduction and extra-life cards as the run gets more dangerous. This balance keeps progression efficient while still protecting long runs.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Shibuya Gadget Priority
items.label: Control And Defense
items.description: Black Hole is excellent for pulling enemies into one kill zone, while Infinity gives a defensive window when the screen becomes crowded. These two gadgets fit Shibuya's fast, open-wave style especially well.

animeApocalypseImpelDownGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Impel Down Guide
animeApocalypseImpelDownGuide.subtitle: Learn how to move through Anime Apocalypse Impel Down prison rooms, underground sections, lava visuals, and demanding dungeon routes without losing tempo.
animeApocalypseImpelDownGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse Impel Down is a darker prison-themed dungeon with underground sections, lava-floor visuals, and more demanding traversal than the open Shibuya layout. The key to clearing it smoothly is to use movement tools aggressively while slowing down near boss rooms, hazards, and confusing exits.

items.title: Enter Anime Apocalypse Impel Down after learning core movement
items.description: Start with easier rooms or Shibuya-style routes until double jump, dash, slide, and wall run feel natural. Anime Apocalypse Impel Down is better when you can reposition during zombie waves instead of fighting every group in place.
items.playerGoal: Reach rooms faster, avoid getting boxed in, and keep enough space to cycle skills.

items.title: Read the Anime Apocalypse prison layout before committing
items.description: Anime Apocalypse Impel Down uses prison-inspired routes with underground sections and tighter room flow. Before using long cooldown skills, check enemy positions, nearby walls, corners, and the visible exit direction so your damage lands from a useful angle.
items.playerGoal: Use the room shape to group zombies instead of letting enemies surround you.

items.title: Handle Anime Apocalypse lava visuals with steady movement
items.description: Some lava visuals in Anime Apocalypse Impel Down are part of the environment and can be crossed or used for traversal. Treat lava areas as movement checks: test safe paths, keep momentum, and use wall-running surfaces when the route supports it.
items.playerGoal: Move through visual hazard zones without stopping your clear speed.

items.title: Use Anime Apocalypse wall running to fix awkward routes
items.description: Vertical surfaces can be used for wall running, letting you reach elevated spots, cross parts of a room, or recover from a bad position. This is especially useful in prison-style rooms where the ground path is crowded by zombies.
items.playerGoal: Escape pressure, skip slow floor routes, and reposition before enemies catch up.

items.title: Clear Anime Apocalypse Impel Down rooms with cooldown cycling
items.description: Open with a grouping tool or high-area skill, then rotate normal attacks, dash movement, and lower-cooldown skills while waiting for your strongest abilities. Cooldown reduction cards and upgrades make Anime Apocalypse Impel Down smoother because they reduce dead time between waves.
items.playerGoal: Keep damage uptime high while avoiding face-tanking large groups.

items.title: Approach Anime Apocalypse boss rooms carefully
items.description: Dying in dungeon content can force a restart from the beginning, so avoid rushing boss-room entrances when your cooldowns, gadget, or ultimate are unavailable. Enter with space, save a defensive tool when possible, and use dash or wall movement before committing to long animations.
items.playerGoal: Finish the dungeon without losing the whole run to one bad room.

animeApocalypseFarmingGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Farming Guide
animeApocalypseFarmingGuide.subtitle: Compare Anime Apocalypse coins, drops, spins, raid tickets, mastery progress, and battle pass rewards before pushing harder dungeons or Infinite mode.
animeApocalypseFarmingGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse progression is built around repeat runs, card choices, codes, quests, spins, upgrades, and long-term account systems. The fastest early progression comes from stacking economy rewards first, then converting those rewards into better abilities, gadgets, traits, cooldown upgrades, and stronger run consistency.

items.bestUse: Spend coins on core Anime Apocalypse upgrades first, especially cooldown speed and walk speed, before chasing luxury rolls.
items.farmingTip: Pick Wealth early in a run when your damage is stable, then switch toward cooldown or survival cards once your economy is rolling.

items.bestUse: Use drops as the backbone for Anime Apocalypse item collection and cosmetic crafting.
items.farmingTip: Run content while drop boosts are active and avoid skipping glowing ground pickups, because drops can be easy to miss during fast movement.

items.bestUse: Roll for usable Anime Apocalypse abilities before investing heavily into mastery or trait rerolls.
items.farmingTip: Use normal spins to build a playable ability base, then save higher-value resources for chasing rare upgrades.

items.bestUse: Use Lucky Ability Spins when you are actively chasing higher-rarity Anime Apocalypse abilities such as Mythic or Supreme-tier options.
items.farmingTip: Redeem spin-heavy codes before long play sessions so the rewards immediately improve your farming setup.

items.bestUse: Roll for a practical Anime Apocalypse gadget that supports wave clearing, grouping, or defense.
items.farmingTip: A strong gadget can stabilize runs even before your ability roster is perfect, so do not ignore gadget spins.

items.bestUse: Chase high-impact Anime Apocalypse gadgets such as Black Hole or Infinity for better crowd control and survival.
items.farmingTip: Use Lucky Gadget Spins when your current ability is already serviceable and your main weakness is wave control or safety.

items.bestUse: Save raid tickets for Anime Apocalypse attempts where your ability, gadget, and upgrades are already strong enough to get value from the run.
items.farmingTip: The SORRYFORDELAY release reward includes 20 Raid Tickets, making code redemption an important first step before raid-focused play.

bestSources: Repeated runs with the same ability
items.bestUse: Build mastery on Anime Apocalypse abilities you actually plan to use, since mastery progression can unlock additional ability value.
items.farmingTip: Do not spread mastery across too many abilities early; focus on one reliable farming ability until your core build is stable.

items.bestUse: Claim Anime Apocalypse seasonal rewards such as spins, gold, and the Fire Fighter battle pass weapon when available.
items.farmingTip: Treat battle pass progress as a bonus layered on top of normal farming, because your seasonal level contributes to pass rewards.

items.bestUse: Apply trait rerolls to Anime Apocalypse abilities you are likely to keep instead of rerolling weak temporary weapons.
items.farmingTip: Save rerolls until you have a strong ability base, then chase traits that improve damage, speed, or run consistency.

animeApocalypsePetsTitlesCosmeticsGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Pets, Titles, and Cosmetics Guide
animeApocalypsePetsTitlesCosmeticsGuide.subtitle: Understand the Anime Apocalypse systems outside your main ability and gadget, including pets, titles, items, cosmetics, traits, and crafting.
animeApocalypsePetsTitlesCosmeticsGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse is not only about rolling a strong ability. The game also includes pets, titles, traits, items, cosmetics, quests, and reward systems that add stat bonuses, crafting goals, and long-term reasons to keep clearing zombies.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Pets
items.summary: Pets follow your character and grant bonus stats.
items.content: Anime Apocalypse pets are part of the long-term progression loop and are worth farming because they add account power beyond your active ability. Since the gameplay loop lets you earn loot and currency from maps, pets should be treated as a parallel upgrade path while you continue improving abilities and gadgets.
items.bestFor: Players who want passive power while farming dungeons or Infinite mode.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Titles
items.summary: Titles are equipable progression rewards that provide additional stat buffs.
items.content: Anime Apocalypse titles are useful because they let players add extra stats without changing their core ability or gadget setup. Equip a title that matches your current weakness, such as survivability, speed, or damage-focused progression.
items.bestFor: Players optimizing their build after basic ability and gadget choices are already working.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Items
items.summary: Items can be obtained from defeated zombies and feed into the crafting loop.
items.content: Anime Apocalypse zombie drops are important because items connect regular combat to cosmetic crafting. Pick up glowing ground drops during fast clears so that dungeon and wave farming also builds your item inventory over time.
items.bestFor: Players farming repeated dungeon runs and collecting materials while progressing.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Cosmetics
items.summary: Cosmetics are equipment pieces that can grant additional stats.
items.content: Anime Apocalypse cosmetics are not just visual collection goals. They are listed as equipment with stat value, so players should treat cosmetic crafting as part of account progression rather than a purely decorative system.
items.bestFor: Players who want extra stat layers after improving abilities, gadgets, pets, and titles.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Cosmetic Crafting
items.summary: Crafting connects zombie drops, items, and cosmetic progression.
items.content: Defeating Anime Apocalypse zombies produces items that can be used for cosmetic crafting. The best approach is to farm content you can clear consistently, collect drops every run, and craft cosmetics once your inventory supports the target piece.
items.bestFor: Players who farm repeated rooms and want long-term goals beyond spins.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Weapon Skins
items.summary: Weapon skins can change the visual effects of a weapon.
items.content: Anime Apocalypse weapon skins are part of the cosmetic layer and can make a favorite ability feel more distinctive. They are best treated as style upgrades after your combat foundation is already stable.
items.bestFor: Players who want a cleaner look or stronger anime-style presentation for their main weapon.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Traits
items.summary: Traits are passive modifiers that make abilities stronger.
items.content: Anime Apocalypse traits are rolled onto abilities and should be used carefully because trait rerolls are valuable. Save rerolls for abilities you plan to keep, then use traits to reinforce your preferred farming or survival style.
items.bestFor: Players who already have a strong ability and want to optimize it further.

animeApocalypseControlsMovementGuide.title: Anime Apocalypse Controls and Movement Guide
animeApocalypseControlsMovementGuide.subtitle: Master Anime Apocalypse attacking, skills, gadgets, ultimate usage, dodging, sliding, double jumping, air dashing, and wall running.
animeApocalypseControlsMovementGuide.intro: Anime Apocalypse plays faster than a standard wave-defense Roblox game because combat is built around constant repositioning. Learning the controls early makes every system easier: you clear rooms faster, pick up drops more reliably, avoid boss pressure, and survive longer in endless zombie waves.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Basic Movement
items.description: Use standard Roblox movement to circle enemies, kite zombie groups, and stay out of corners. Never stand still after starting a wave because enemy pressure scales quickly in crowded rooms.
items.comboTip: Move sideways while attacking so zombies group in front of you instead of surrounding you.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Jump and Double Jump
items.description: Jump is the base of the movement chain, and Anime Apocalypse supports double-jump style mobility. Use jumping to cross room gaps, reset your position, and prepare for air dash or wall-running routes.
items.comboTip: Jump before dashing when you need distance instead of a short ground dodge.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Dash and Dodge
items.description: Dash is your main escape and repositioning tool. Use it to dodge attacks, leave crowded enemy clusters, and create space before cycling your next skill.
items.comboTip: Dash after a skill animation to avoid being punished while cooldowns recover.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Slide
items.description: Slide gives another movement option for quick repositioning on the ground. It is useful when you need to slip out of pressure without spending a bigger combat action.
items.comboTip: Slide through open lanes, then turn and use an area skill once enemies have grouped behind you.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Basic Attack
items.description: Basic attack fills the gaps between ability cooldowns. It is not just filler; it keeps damage moving while you wait for skills, gadget, or ultimate to come back.
items.comboTip: Use basic attacks while kiting instead of waiting passively for cooldowns.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Ability Skills
items.description: Ability skills are your main damage buttons and define your combat style. Rotate them instead of using everything at once, especially in demanding rooms where cooldown timing matters.
items.comboTip: Open with a wide or grouping skill, then save one skill for enemies that spawn behind you.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Gadget
items.description: Gadgets are secondary active tools that support your ability. Use offensive gadgets to group or clear zombies, and defensive gadgets when you need time to reset positioning.
items.comboTip: Use a crowd-control gadget before your strongest area skill for cleaner wave clears.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Ultimate
items.description: Ultimate skills are high-impact abilities best saved for boss pressure, large zombie waves, or emergency clears. Do not waste an ultimate on a nearly finished room unless it prevents a death.
items.comboTip: Enter harder rooms with ultimate ready when possible, especially on Impel Down or higher difficulty runs.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Air Dash
items.description: Air dash lets you rapidly reposition after jumping and is one of the fastest ways to escape enemy pressure. It also helps you cross larger room sections and maintain speed during farming routes.
items.comboTip: Double jump into air dash when a ground route is blocked by zombies.

items.title: Anime Apocalypse Wall Run
items.description: Wall running lets you move along vertical surfaces, reach elevated routes, and skip awkward parts of a room. This is especially valuable in maps with tighter paths or environmental hazards.
items.comboTip: Wall run to escape crowded floors, then drop back into a better attack angle.

faq.title: Anime Apocalypse FAQ
faq.subtitle: Answers for Anime Apocalypse codes, abilities, gadgets, maps, and first-run progression.

questions.question: What is Anime Apocalypse on Roblox?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse is a Roblox anime zombie roguelike survival game where players clear waves, roll abilities and gadgets, pick cards, farm rewards, and improve their builds.

questions.question: How do Anime Apocalypse codes help new players?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse codes give coins, spins, boost timers, raid tickets, and trait rerolls that speed up early ability rolls, gadget rolls, upgrades, and farming sessions.

questions.question: Which Anime Apocalypse ability should beginners use first?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse beginners should learn the starter Zorro kit first, then keep a smoother roll such as Coyote, Shadow Monarch, Stark, or another ability that clears waves consistently.

questions.question: How does the Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha work?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse Ability Gacha uses Normal Spins and Lucky Spins, includes Rare through Supreme rarities, and has pity milestones at 50 rolls for Legendary and 100 rolls for Mythic.

questions.question: What maps should Anime Apocalypse beginners start with?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse beginners should start with Shibuya for cleaner room flow, then move into Impel Down after learning dash timing, double jump, air dash, and wall movement.

questions.question: What should Anime Apocalypse players upgrade early?
questions.answer: Anime Apocalypse players should prioritize cooldown speed, movement comfort, ability access, gadget support, and economy tools so each run clears rooms faster and survives longer.
